# base64.dev

> base64.dev is a fast, privacy-first Base64 encoder and decoder for developers. No ads, no tracking, no sign-up. All encoding happens locally in the browser.

## Tool

- [Base64 Encoder & Decoder](https://base64.dev/): The main tool. Supports text, image, file, and URL-safe Base64 modes. Auto-detects whether input is plain text or Base64. Keyboard shortcuts included.
- [All Tools & Converters](https://base64.dev/tools): Index of every base64.dev converter, calculator, and per-language encode/decode tool.

## Converters (in-browser tools)

- [Image to Base64](https://base64.dev/image-to-base64): Drop a PNG/JPG/GIF/SVG/WebP image and get a Base64 data URI for HTML or CSS. Runs locally, no upload.
- [Base64 to Image](https://base64.dev/base64-to-image): Decode a Base64 string or data URI back into a previewable, downloadable image. Auto-detects the format from the bytes.
- [Base64 to PDF](https://base64.dev/base64-to-pdf): Decode Base64 into a PDF, preview it inline, and download it. Fully client-side — safe for confidential documents.
- [Base64 to Hex](https://base64.dev/base64-to-hex): Convert a Base64 string to the hexadecimal representation of its bytes, with optional spaced/uppercase output.
- [Hex to Base64](https://base64.dev/hex-to-base64): Convert a hex string (spaces, colons, and 0x prefixes accepted) to standard or URL-safe Base64.
- [Base64 to JSON](https://base64.dev/base64-to-json): Decode Base64 (or base64url) to text and pretty-print it as JSON. Handles JWT payload segments.
- [File to Base64](https://base64.dev/file-to-base64): Encode any file (PDF, binary, archive, image) to a raw Base64 string or data URI. Runs locally.
- [Base64 to File](https://base64.dev/base64-to-file): Decode any Base64 string and download it as a file, with the type auto-detected from the bytes.
- [PNG to Base64](https://base64.dev/png-to-base64): Convert a PNG to a Base64 data URI for HTML/CSS.
- [JPG to Base64](https://base64.dev/jpg-to-base64): Convert a JPG/JPEG to a Base64 data URI.
- [SVG to Base64](https://base64.dev/svg-to-base64): Convert an SVG to a Base64 data URI for CSS background-image (with the URL-encoding tradeoff explained).
- [PDF to Base64](https://base64.dev/pdf-to-base64): Encode a PDF to a Base64 string or data URI.
- [MP3 / Audio to Base64](https://base64.dev/mp3-to-base64): Encode MP3/WAV/OGG audio to a Base64 string or data URI.
- [Base64 to PNG](https://base64.dev/base64-to-png): Decode a Base64 string or data URI into a downloadable PNG image.
- [Base64 to SVG](https://base64.dev/base64-to-svg): Decode Base64 (or a data URI) to SVG source with a live preview and download.
- [Base64 to CSV](https://base64.dev/base64-to-csv): Decode Base64 to CSV text with a live table preview and download.
- [Base64 to Blob](https://base64.dev/base64-to-blob): Convert Base64 to a JavaScript Blob and download it — includes the canonical base64ToBlob() code.
- [Data URI Generator](https://base64.dev/data-uri-generator): Turn text (with a MIME type) or any dropped file into a data: URI for inlining in HTML/CSS.

## Calculators & utilities

- [Base64 Size Calculator](https://base64.dev/base64-size-calculator): Compute Base64 size and overhead. Bytes ↔ Base64 length, the exact ~33% increase, formula explained.
- [Base64URL Encode & Decode](https://base64.dev/base64url): URL-safe Base64 (RFC 4648 §5) with the -_ alphabet and no padding — for JWTs, URLs, and filenames.

## Decoders & developer tools

- [JWT Decoder](https://base64.dev/jwt-decoder): Decode a JSON Web Token's Base64URL header and payload to JSON, with humanized exp/iat/nbf claims. Signature is not verified; runs locally.
- [Basic Auth Header Generator](https://base64.dev/basic-auth-header-generator): Build an `Authorization: Basic base64(user:pass)` header from a username and password, and decode existing headers. Base64 is encoding, not encryption.
- [Hex Decoder](https://base64.dev/hex-decode): Decode a hexadecimal string to UTF-8 text, or encode text to hex.
- [UTF-8 Decoder](https://base64.dev/utf-8-decode): Decode UTF-8 bytes (hex or percent-encoded) to text, or inspect the UTF-8 bytes of any string.
- [HTML Entity Decoder](https://base64.dev/html-entity-decode): Decode HTML entities (named and numeric) to text, or encode text into entities. Decoding is done in a detached element, nothing renders.
- [URL Decode](https://base64.dev/url-decode): Percent-decode a URL-encoded string, with an optional "+ as space" mode.
- [URL Encode](https://base64.dev/url-encode): Percent-encode text with encodeURIComponent (component) or encodeURI (full URI).

## Encode & decode by language (live tool + code)

Each page pairs an in-browser encoder/decoder with the canonical, copy-paste Base64 code for that language.

- [Python](https://base64.dev/base64-encode-python): base64.b64encode / b64decode, URL-safe, files.
- [JavaScript](https://base64.dev/base64-encode-javascript): btoa / atob with the TextEncoder Unicode-safe pattern.
- [TypeScript](https://base64.dev/base64-encode-typescript): type-safe wrappers, browser vs Node Buffer.
- [Node.js](https://base64.dev/base64-encode-nodejs): Buffer.from(...).toString('base64') and 'base64url'.
- [Java](https://base64.dev/base64-encode-java): java.util.Base64 getEncoder / getUrlEncoder.
- [Go](https://base64.dev/base64-encode-go): encoding/base64 StdEncoding / URLEncoding / RawURLEncoding.
- [Rust](https://base64.dev/base64-encode-rust): the base64 crate Engine API (general_purpose::STANDARD).
- [PHP](https://base64.dev/base64-encode-php): base64_encode / base64_decode and the URL-safe variant.
- [C#](https://base64.dev/base64-encode-csharp): Convert.ToBase64String / FromBase64String with Encoding.UTF8.
- [Ruby](https://base64.dev/base64-encode-ruby): strict_encode64 vs encode64, urlsafe_encode64.
- [Swift](https://base64.dev/base64-encode-swift): Data.base64EncodedString() / Data(base64Encoded:).
- [Kotlin](https://base64.dev/base64-encode-kotlin): kotlin.io.encoding.Base64, java.util.Base64, android.util.Base64.
- [C++](https://base64.dev/base64-encode-cpp): self-contained std::string encoder + OpenSSL EVP.
- [C](https://base64.dev/base64-encode-c): portable lookup-table encoder/decoder + OpenSSL EVP.
- [Bash](https://base64.dev/base64-encode-bash): the base64 command, echo -n gotcha, -w 0 line wrapping.
- [PowerShell](https://base64.dev/base64-encode-powershell): [Convert]::ToBase64String with the UTF-16 vs UTF-8 trap.
- [Dart & Flutter](https://base64.dev/base64-encode-dart): dart:convert base64Encode / base64Decode / base64Url.
- [Perl](https://base64.dev/base64-encode-perl): MIME::Base64 encode_base64 / decode_base64, one-liners, the newline gotcha.

## Guides & Articles

- [What is Base64 Encoding?](https://base64.dev/articles/what-is-base64): Complete explanation of the Base64 scheme, the 64-character alphabet, padding, size overhead, and use cases.
- [URL-Safe Base64 (RFC 4648)](https://base64.dev/articles/url-safe-base64): How URL-safe Base64 differs from standard Base64, when to use it, and how to convert between them.
- [Base64 Image Encoding & Data URIs](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-image-encoding): How to embed images in HTML/CSS as Base64 data URIs, with code examples and performance tradeoffs.
- [Base64 in JWT](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-jwt): How JSON Web Tokens use Base64URL encoding for headers, payloads, and signatures.
- [Base64 vs Hex Encoding](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-vs-hex): Size comparison, use case breakdown, and when to choose each format.
- [Base64 vs Base64URL](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-vs-base64url): The +/ vs -_ character swap, padding differences, and when to use the URL-safe variant (JWTs, URLs, filenames).
- [Is Base64 Encryption?](https://base64.dev/articles/is-base64-encryption): No — Base64 is encoding, not encryption. Encoding vs encryption vs hashing, why Base64 hides nothing, and how to actually protect data.
- [Base64 Data URIs Explained](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-data-uri): The data: URI scheme in depth — syntax, MIME types, browser limits, and performance.

## Language Guides

- [Base64 in JavaScript](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-javascript): btoa(), atob(), Unicode handling with TextEncoder, URL-safe Base64, and encoding files.
- [Base64 in TypeScript](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-typescript): Type-safe Base64 utilities for browser and Node.js, including Zod validation schemas.
- [Base64 in Node.js](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-nodejs): Buffer-based encoding/decoding, streams, URL-safe mode, and file handling in Node.js.
- [Base64 in Python](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-python): The base64 module, b64encode/b64decode, URL-safe variants, and encoding files.
- [Base64 in Java](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-java): java.util.Base64 — basic, URL-safe, and MIME encoders in Java 8+.
- [Base64 in Go](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-go): encoding/base64 package — StdEncoding, URLEncoding, RawURLEncoding, and streaming.
- [Base64 in Rust](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-rust): The base64 crate Engine API — standard, URL-safe, and custom alphabets.
- [Base64 in PHP](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-php): base64_encode(), base64_decode(), URL-safe variant, and encoding files.
- [Base64 in C# (.NET)](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-csharp): Convert.ToBase64String, span-based APIs, URL-safe Base64, and stream encoding.
- [Base64 in Ruby](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-ruby): encode64 vs strict_encode64 vs urlsafe_encode64 and the critical differences between them.
- [Base64 in Swift](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-swift): Data.base64EncodedString(), URL-safe extension, encoding images for iOS/macOS.
- [Base64 in Kotlin](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-kotlin): java.util.Base64 on JVM/Android, android.util.Base64 for older API levels, Kotlin Multiplatform.
- [Base64 in C](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-c): complete lookup-table encoder/decoder, URL-safe variant, and OpenSSL EVP API alternative.
- [Base64 in C++](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-cpp): std::string implementation, Boost.Beast, OpenSSL EVP API, and URL-safe variant.
- [Base64 in Dart & Flutter](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-dart): dart:convert base64 and base64Url codecs, image encoding, and HTTP usage.
- [Base64 in React (File Upload)](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-react): FileReader API, image preview, sending Base64 to APIs, and TypeScript types.
- [Base64 in PowerShell](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-powershell): [Convert]::ToBase64String, the UTF-16 encoding trap, and cross-platform usage.
- [Base64 in Bash Scripts](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-bash): the echo -n newline gotcha, file encoding, env variables, and multi-line handling.

## Concepts & Troubleshooting

- [Base64 Padding Explained](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-padding): why = signs exist, how padding is calculated, and when it can be omitted in JWTs and URL-safe Base64.
- [Fix Invalid Base64 & Padding Errors](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-invalid-padding): diagnose and fix decode failures from missing padding, whitespace, and wrong variant.
- [Base64 vs Base32, Base58 & Base85](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-encodings-comparison): size overhead, alphabets, and when to choose each encoding scheme.
- [HTTP Basic Auth & Base64](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-basic-auth): credentials in the Authorization header, with security warnings.
- [Binary Data in JSON with Base64](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-json): how to embed binary in JSON, size tradeoffs, and multipart alternatives.

## CLI & Tools

- [base64 Command in Linux & macOS](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-linux-mac): GNU vs BSD flags, -w 0 line wrapping, and shell pipeline patterns.
- [OpenSSL Base64](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-openssl): encode and decode with openssl enc -base64, the -A single-line flag, and certificate workflows.
- [Base64 for SSH Keys & PEM Certificates](https://base64.dev/articles/base64-ssh-pem): encode private keys and certs for env vars, Docker, Kubernetes, and CI/CD pipelines.

## About

- [About base64.dev](https://base64.dev/about): How and why base64.dev was built. Tech stack: vanilla HTML/CSS/JS, no frameworks, deployed on Vercel.
